question_title: Will Apple publicly ship any of the delayed “personalized Siri” features before May 1, 2026?
summary: The personalized Siri features (personal context, onscreen awareness, cross-app actions) are not in any current public iOS release, not in the iOS 26.5 beta (released March 30, 2026), and all credible reporting from Bloomberg, MacRumors, TechCrunch, and 9to5Mac indicates these features have been pushed to iOS 27 (September 2026). iOS 26.5's public release is expected mid-May 2026, which is after the May 1 deadline. There is no plausible release vehicle for these features before the resolution date. The 2% accounts for radical uncertainty (e.g., all reporting is wrong and Apple makes an unprecedented surprise release).

factors.description: iOS 26.5 beta 1 (March 30, 2026) contains zero personalized Siri features — features aren't even in the pipeline for the next release
iOS 26.5 public release expected mid-May 2026 (week of May 11-18), AFTER the May 1 deadline — no release vehicle exists before the deadline
Multiple credible outlets (MacRumors, Bloomberg, TechCrunch) report features pushed to iOS 27 (September 2026)
iOS 26.4 is the current public release (March 24, 2026) and definitively lacks personalized Siri features
Theoretical possibility of surprise 26.4.x point update with Siri features — contradicts all reporting and Apple's historical release patterns

reasoning:
**Forecast: 2% (YES)**

The evidence is overwhelmingly against resolution before May 1, 2026:

- **No features in current release**: iOS 26.4 (released March 24) doesn't include any personalized Siri features.
- **No features in beta**: iOS 26.5 beta 1 (March 30) has zero new Siri functionality.
- **No release vehicle before deadline**: iOS 26.5 public release is expected mid-May (week of May 11-18), after the May 1 cutoff.
- **Consensus reporting**: Bloomberg, MacRumors, TechCrunch, and 9to5Mac all indicate personalized Siri features have been pushed to iOS 27 (September 2026).
- **Only ~20 days remain** before the resolution date, with no indication Apple is preparing any surprise release.

The 2% residual probability accounts for the extreme tail scenario where all reporting is wrong and Apple makes an unprecedented move.
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will Apple publicly ship any of the delayed “personalized Siri” features before May 1, 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/43126)
[Apple Confirms Revamped Siri is Still Coming in 2026](https://www.macrumors.com/2026/02/12/siri-ios-26-launch-confirmed-apple/)
[New Siri Features Absent From iOS 26.5 Beta, Likely Pushed to iOS 27 in September](https://www.macrumors.com/2026/03/30/ios-26-5-no-new-siri-features/)
[iOS 26.5 release date: Here’s when to expect new iPhone features - 9to5Mac](https://9to5mac.com/2026/04/02/ios-26-5-release-date-heres-when-to-expect-new-iphone-features/)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves Yes if, before May 1, 2026, Apple officially says that at least one of the delayed personalized Siri capabilities is available to users in a public non-beta release. Otherwise it resolves No.
fine_print:
For this question, the delayed “personalized Siri” features are limited to the Siri capabilities Apple described in 2024 as:

using a user’s personal context to answer or act on requests;
onscreen awareness enabling Siri to understand and act on content currently displayed; or
taking actions in and across apps in the more capable Siri experience Apple said would come later.
Other Apple Intelligence features do not count unless Apple explicitly presents them as one of these Siri capabilities.
